title: Subsidy for weeds: why does Romania pay millions for bare land? We prefer to treat, not to prevent
summary: The article criticizes Romania's agricultural subsidy system, which rewards land ownership rather than actual production, allowing landowners to collect EU funds without generating any agricultural output. It argues for raising eligibility thresholds, investing in food processing infrastructure, replacing post-disaster compensation with insurance systems, and fiscalizing EU funds above €5,000 to ensure accountability and competitiveness.
talking_points: Romania's per-hectare subsidy system rewards land ownership equally whether the land produces crops or weeds, undermining productive farmers
Romania remains trapped as a raw material exporter, selling cheap grain and importing expensive processed food products
Post-drought compensation keeps farmers dependent on government aid instead of building resilience through insurance and irrigation systems
EU funds exceeding €5,000 should be fiscalized to ensure accountability and productive use of hundreds of millions of euros
The eligibility threshold for subsidies should increase from 1 hectare to at least 3-5 hectares to filter out non-productive recipients
author: Sebastian Cernic
